page: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. It features 88 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 88 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance distinction between single-core and multi-core operations. While single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range, multi-core performance is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier. The server demonstrates exceptional memory bandwidth, exceeding 11,000 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 469 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also excels in specialized compute workloads, achieving top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the h3-standard-88 offers high resource density for parallelized workloads that can leverage its 88 dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its AMX instructions provide strong prompt processing capabilities. Conversely, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server featuring 88 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 352 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal average single-core performance but top-tier multi-core capabilities, supported by exceptional memory bandwidth and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. This hardware profile delivers high resource density for parallelized tasks, making it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing. However, the absence of local storage requires reliance on network-attached disks, which is a key tradeoff for I/O-intensive applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 features 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, making it ideal for parallelized database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 offers 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 352 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 88-core x86_64 platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 88 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores with 352 GB RAM
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated compute
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
